pi_items.source: OpenBCI GUI source code reference showing TimeSeriesYLim.UV_200 as a built-in scale option; GitHub issue discussions confirm 200 μV is a standard preset vertical scale option

GitHub issue #419 ('Time Series Widget: Setting window to 7 sec...') and search results confirm time window options include seconds-based values; maximum buffer is 20 seconds, suggesting 10 seconds is a valid intermediate option
setup_task.sh creates the Screenshots directory at line 18: 'su - ga -c "mkdir -p /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots"'
Process name confirmed throughout all scripts: setup_task.sh (line 10 'pkill -f "OpenBCI_GUI"'), export_result.sh (line 28 'pgrep -f "OpenBCI_GUI"'), install_openbci.sh (line 23 'echo "OpenBCI_GUI"')

privileged_info_summary: The task configures the OpenBCI GUI Time Series widget with two specific settings: Vertical Scale set to 200 microvolts (μV) and Time Window set to 10 seconds. Both are standard configuration options in OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023). The task uses a Synthetic (Cyton) data session generated by the OpenBCI GUI application, not real PhysioNet data files. The environment installs OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 via GitHub release and downloads real EEG data from PhysioNet's eegmmidb dataset (S001R01 and S001R04 files) for use by other tasks. The screenshot destination directory is created in /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/.
